channel.title: Cost Savings With Vitamin D on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Cost Savings With Vitamin D on VitaminDWiki

item.title: The Dutch are no longer re-embursed for Vitamin D
item.description:
Monitoring the vitamin D package measure

  • “…discontinue the reimbursement of all vitamin D products containing cholecalciferol or calcifediol from 1 January 2023”

People belong to this vulnerable group if they have at least 1 of these characteristics. This was selected because people often have more than 1 issue.

people with difficulty keeping oversight of their medicines;

people with osteoporosis;

people with a language barrier or an immigration background;

people with a low level of education;

item.description:

item.title: US Healthcare spending to consume 20% of GDP by 2028
item.description:
• Modern HealthCare March 2022
      • It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Modern HealthCare March 2022

It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly


Markdown:
--------
{{< toc >}}

---

#### [Modern HealthCare](https://www.modernhealthcare.com/healthcare-economics/healthcare-spending-consume-20-gdp-2028#:~:text=As%20a%20result%2C%20the%20healthcare,which%20was%2017.7%25%20of%20GDP.) March 2022

---

#### It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

 **Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly** 



AST Structure:
-------------
├── DivNode
│   full_match: `{DIV(class="lefth4")}{maketoc Title=""}{DIV}`
│   inner_content: `{maketoc Title=""}`
│   attrs_dict:

│   │   class: lefth4
│   children:
│   ├── MakeTocNode
│   │   full_match: `{maketoc Title=""}`
│   │   inner_content: ``
│   │   attrs_dict:
│   │   │   raw_content:  Title=""

item.title: Estimated cost savings of getting most Canadians to 40 ng of Vitamin D - 2016
item.description:
• Considering: Increasing the vitamin D level of all Canadians to 40 ng would save 6 to 18 billion dollars a year – Grant Nov 2016
      • The ROI DOES NOT consider non-medical cost savings from additional Vitamin D:
      • The ROI DOES NOT consider the cost savings due to Magnesium or Vitamin K2

Considering: Increasing the vitamin D level of all Canadians to 40 ng would save 6 to 18 billion dollars a year – Grant Nov 2016

It estimated at the medical cost savings from just 7 health problems by an average of 20%

item.title: Severe asthmatics willing to pay 5180 dollars for relief, Vitamin D needed costs only 45 dollars
item.description:
• Vitamin D supplementation for children with mild to moderate asthma: an economic evaluation
      • Overview Asthma and Vitamin D contains
      • VitaminDWiki -Less Asthma if mother took vitamin D while pregnant – Oct 2023

Vitamin D supplementation for children with mild to moderate asthma: an economic evaluation

Journal of Asthma Volume 60, 2023 - Issue 9 https://doi.org/10.1080/02770903.2023.2178007 PDF behind paywall

item.title: People with extremely low vitamin D levels consume 2X more health care dollars – 2018
item.description:
• Vitamin D and health care costs: Results from two independent population-based cohort studies
      • VitaminDWiki - VA found less testing for vitamin D resulted in increased health costs – Jan 2012

Vitamin D and health care costs: Results from two independent population-based cohort studies

Clinical Nutrition Volume 37, Issue 6, Part A, December 2018, Pages 2149-2155 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.clnu.2017.10.014

item.title: Depression would be cost-effectively decreased in teens by Vitamin D (Iran)
item.description:
• Is vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian schools effective in reducing adolescent depressive symptoms? Cost effectiveness study

Is vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian schools effective in reducing adolescent depressive symptoms? Cost effectiveness study

BMC Public Health . 2023 Jul 20;23(1):1393. doi: 10.1186/s12889-023-16244-z. PDF behind paywall

Seyed Vahid Jasemi 1, Zhale Zandieh 2, Narges Zandieh 1, Mohsen Rezaei Hemami 3, Ali Darvishi 4 5, Zahra Abdollahi 6, Ramin Heshmat 7

Purpose: We aimed to assess the cost-effectiveness of the vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian adolescents reducing adolescent depressive Symptoms.

item.title: Vitamin D has been found to be cost-effective by many studies
item.description:
• VitaminDWiki - The top 10 medical expenses can all be decreased by vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - IVF 4X more successful for white women with lots of vitamin D – many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - Prevent a COVID death: 9 dollars of Vitamin D or 900,000 dollars of vaccine - Aug 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Getting all Canadians to 40 ng would save about $12 billion annually
      • 57 VitaminDWiki catgories are associated with the category Cost-Effective

VitaminDWiki - The top 10 medical expenses can all be decreased by vitamin D

Heart Conditions, Diabetes, Cancer, Obesity, COPD and Asthma, Traffic Injuries, Mental Disorders, Depression; Brain, Low Back and Neck Problems, Joint Disorders, High Blood Pressure,

item.title: Rotator Cuff repair using Vitamin D lowers medical cost by 46 dollars
item.description:
• Preoperative Vitamin D Supplementation is a Cost-Effective Intervention in Arthroscopic Rotator Cuff Repair
      • Cost savings with Vitamin D contains
      • Loading doses should be completed 3+ days before any surgery

Preoperative Vitamin D Supplementation is a Cost-Effective Intervention in Arthroscopic Rotator Cuff Repair

J Shoulder Elbow Surg. 2023 Jun 10 doi: 10.1016/j.jse.2023.05.007   $28 PDF

Dhiraj Patel 1, Gregory Roy 2, Nathan Endres 2, Chason Ziino 2

Background: This study investigates the potential role of preoperative 25(OH)D supplementation as a cost-effective strategy to decrease revision rotator cuff repair (RCR) rates and lower the total healthcare burden from patients undergoing primary arthroscopic RCR. Previous literature has emphasized the importance of vitamin D on bone health maintenance, soft tissue healing, and outcomes in RCR. Inadequate preoperative vitamin D levels may increase revision RCR rates following primary arthroscopic RCR. Although 25(OH)D deficiency is common in RCR patients, serum screening is not routinely performed.

item.title: Reduce 4 health problems by a quarter if increase Vitamin D to 30 ng – Saudi Arabia, Grant
item.description:
• Evidence That Increasing Serum 25(OH)D Concentrations to 30 ng/mL in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates Could Greatly Improve Health Outcomes
      • Getting most of the population >50 ng would provide far more benefits
      • Saudi Arabia needs Magnesium (most of their water has been desalinated)
      • Can get more Vitamin D from the sun without the heat (silver-coated plexiglass)

Evidence That Increasing Serum 25(OH)D Concentrations to 30 ng/mL in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates Could Greatly Improve Health Outcomes

Biomedicines 2023, 11(4), 994; https://doi.org/10.3390/biomedicines11040994
